Q5 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following parameter is not included in the activation record of recursive function call?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Global variables section is included in the “main” activation record of the program. All activation record makes access link to the “main” to access the global variables.

Q6 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A grammar that has not any epsilon productions and also free from unit productions. The maximum number of reduce moves that can be taken during bottom up evaluation of 25 token string by bottom up parsers is ________.

NAT
Correct answer: 49
Solution

49
Maximum number of reduce moves for n token = 2n – 1
So, for 25 tokens = 2 × 25 – 1 = 49

Q7 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Handle is a sub-string that matches right side of production whose reduction to non-terminal on left sides of production represents one step along the reverse of left-most derivation.
S2 : Viable prefixes of grammar G are those prefixes of right sentential form that appears on stack of shift reduce parsing.
Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Handle is a sub-string that matches right side of production whose reduction to non-terminal on left hand side of production represents one step along the reverse of right-most derivation.
• Viable prefixes are those prefixes of right sentential form that can appear on the stack of a shift reduce parser.

Q9 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following grammar:

Where S and X are non-terminals and a, b are terminals. Which of the following can be the viable prefixes?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(b)
Viable prefixes: Combination of non-terminal and terminal which can be in the stack during parsing is called viable prefix.
A handle X→ b is available. So whenever terminal b is encounter it is popped out before inserting new element into the stack.
Option (a) is wrong because before popping ‘b’ element ‘aab’ is inserted.
Option (b) is correct after ‘b’ no element has inserted.
So, option (c) and (d) also incorrect.

Q10 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(i) Type checking is done during all the phases especially in syntax analysis phase.
(ii) Target code generation phase is machine independent code generation.
(iii) SDT with only synthesized attribute, always have a order of evaluation.
(iv) Symbol table is constructed during analysis part of compiler
Which of the above is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Type checking is done during semantic analysis phase.
• Target code generation is machine specific.
• SDT with only synthesized attribute does not have any cyclic dependency. So always have order of evaluation.
• Symbol table is constructed during the lexical, syntax and semantic analysis phase.

Q13 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is not a token in C language?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
C tokens are:
1. Keywords
2. Identifiers
3. Constants
4. Symbols ( ;, (, ), }, {, etc.)
5. Operators
White spaces are used to separate the tokens but they are not counted as a token. White spaces are ignored while identifying the tokens.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• CLR is most powerful but LALR is most costly.
• Every CFG is not LR since all LR are unambiguous but every CFG is not unambiguous.
• LR(k) does not using back-tracking.
• Every SLR grammar is unambiguous but not every unambiguous grammar is SLR.

Q29 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Assume ×, –, + and / are operators. Precedences and associativity given for those operators as following:
I. + has highest precedence among all operators and is right associative.
II. – has the next highest precedence among the remaining operators and is left associative.
III. ×, / are having equal precedence and are right associative.
+ → Multiplication
/ → Addition
× → Subtraction
– → Division
The output of the given expression 3 × 2 + 10 + 5 – 7 × 6/3 is ________. (Upto 2 decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: -2.28
Solution

-2.28 [-2.3 - -2.2]
